creator: aepalea@gmail.com
text:
On an i386 6.3-RELEASE machine, I just encountered exactly the same problem
as described here, which was put into a "suspended" state 11 April 2007 and
not resolved since.

It appears that after Ruby fails to load the pkgdb due to a permission
error, it troops on regardless and generates a seg-fault. This is somewhat
silly as pkgdb will already prompt me to run "pgdbd -u" appropriately when
required.

pkgdb -F
--->  Checking the package registry database
** You do not own /var/db/pkg. (use -f to force or run as root)

pkg_which could easily incorporate the same test before plunging into the
buggy Ruby runtime, so there is little point in blaming Ruby.

What appears to be the pertinent ktrace output:

 75751 ruby18   CALL  open(0x84a07c0,0x2,0)
 75751 ruby18   NAMI  "/var/db/pkg/pkgdb.db"
 75751 ruby18   RET   open -1 errno 13 Permission denied
 75751 ruby18   CALL  sigprocmask(0x3,0x28137b00,0)
 75751 ruby18   RET   sigprocmask 0
 75751 ruby18   CALL  sigprocmask(0x3,0,0x805311c)
 75751 ruby18   RET   sigprocmask 0
 75751 ruby18   CALL  write(0x2,0x84a06a0,0x11)
 75751 ruby18   GIO   fd 2 wrote 17 bytes
       "Permission denied"
 75751 ruby18   RET   write 17/0x11
 75751 ruby18   CALL  write(0x2,0x809fdf0,0x1)
 75751 ruby18   GIO   fd 2 wrote 1 byte
       "
       "
 75751 ruby18   RET   write 1
 75751 ruby18   CALL  sigprocmask(0x3,0x28137b00,0)
 75751 ruby18   RET   sigprocmask 0
 75751 ruby18   CALL  sigprocmask(0x3,0,0x805311c)
 75751 ruby18   RET   sigprocmask 0
 75751 ruby18   CALL  rmdir(0x8565e00)
 75751 ruby18   NAMI  "/var/tmp/portupgradeVidtNhha"
 75751 ruby18   RET   rmdir 0
 75751 ruby18   CALL  sigaction(0x2,0xbfbfea60,0)
 75751 ruby18   RET   sigaction 0
 75751 ruby18   CALL  stat(0x818f4e0,0xbfbfd3b0)
 75751 ruby18   NAMI  "/var/run/portsdb.lock"
 75751 ruby18   RET   stat -1 errno 2 No such file or directory
 75751 ruby18   CALL  stat(0x818f4e0,0xbfbfd3b0)
 75751 ruby18   NAMI  "/var/run/portsdb.lock"
 75751 ruby18   RET   stat -1 errno 2 No such file or directory
 75751 ruby18   CALL  stat(0x81a7e20,0xbfbfd3b0)
 75751 ruby18   NAMI  "/var/run/pkgdb.db.lock"
 75751 ruby18   RET   stat -1 errno 2 No such file or directory
 75751 ruby18   PSIG  SIGSEGV caught handler=0x28172a48 mask=0x0 code=0xc
 75751 ruby18   CALL  sigprocmask(0x1,0,0x805311c)
 75751 ruby18   RET   sigprocmask 0
 75751 ruby18   CALL  write(0x2,0xbfbfe1a0,0x1f)
 75751 ruby18   GIO   fd 2 wrote 31 bytes
       "/usr/local/sbin/pkg_which:280: "
 75751 ruby18   RET   write 31/0x1f
 75751 ruby18   CALL  write(0x2,0x2810e094,0x6)
 75751 ruby18   GIO   fd 2 wrote 6 bytes
       "[BUG] "
 75751 ruby18   RET   write 6
 75751 ruby18   CALL  write(0x2,0xbfbfdba0,0x12)
 75751 ruby18   GIO   fd 2 wrote 18 bytes
       "Segmentation fault"
 75751 ruby18   RET   write 18/0x12
 75751 ruby18   CALL  write(0x2,0xbfbfdb70,0x2a)
 75751 ruby18   GIO   fd 2 wrote 42 bytes
       "
        ruby 1.8.6 (2007-09-24) [i386-freebsd6]

       "
 75751 ruby18   RET   write 42/0x2a
 75751 ruby18   CALL  sigprocmask(0x3,0xbfbfe158,0)
 75751 ruby18   RET   sigprocmask 0
 75751 ruby18   CALL  sigprocmask(0x3,0,0x805311c)
 75751 ruby18   RET   sigprocmask 0
 75751 ruby18   CALL  getpid
 75751 ruby18   RET   getpid 75751/0x127e7
 75751 ruby18   CALL  kill(0x127e7,0x6)
 75751 ruby18   RET   kill 0
 75751 ruby18   PSIG  SIGIOT SIG_DFL
 75751 ruby18   NAMI  "ruby18.core"
